title: Definition of the State
text:
Constitution of India — Section 12: Definition of the State.
In this Part, unless the context otherwise requires, the State includes the Government and Parliament of India and the Government and the Legislature of each of the States and all local or other authorities within the territory of India or under the control of the Government of India.

title: Right to Equality
text:
Constitution of India — Section 14: Right to Equality.
The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

title: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
text:
Constitution of India — Section 15: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth.
The State shall not discriminate against any citizen on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth or any of them.

title: Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment
text:
Constitution of India — Section 16: Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment.
There shall be equality of opportunity for all citizens in matters relating to employment or appointment to any office under the State.

title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc.
text:
Constitution of India — Section 19: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc..
All citizens shall have the right to freedom of speech and expression; to assemble peaceably and without arms; to form associations or unions; to move freely throughout the territory of India; to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India; and to practice any profession, or to carry on any occupation, trade or business.

title: Protection of life and personal liberty
text:
Constitution of India — Section 21: Protection of life and personal liberty.
No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.

title: Right to education
text:
Constitution of India — Section 21A: Right to education.
The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may, by law, determine.

title: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases
text:
Constitution of India — Section 22: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases.
No person who is arrested shall be detained in custody without being informed of the grounds for such arrest and the right to consult a legal practitioner of his choice.

title: Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion
text:
Constitution of India — Section 25: Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion.
Subject to public order, morality and health, all persons are equally entitled to freedom of conscience and the right freely to profess, practice and propagate religion.

title: Freedom to manage religious affairs
text:
Constitution of India — Section 26: Freedom to manage religious affairs.
Subject to public order, morality and health, every religious denomination or any section thereof shall have the right to manage its own affairs in matters of religion.

title: Freedom as to payment of taxes for promotion of any particular religion
text:
Constitution of India — Section 27: Freedom as to payment of taxes for promotion of any particular religion.
No person shall be compelled to pay any taxes for the promotion or maintenance of any particular religion or religious denomination.

title: Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction or religious worship in certain educational institutions
text:
Constitution of India — Section 28: Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction or religious worship in certain educational institutions.
No religious instruction shall be provided in any educational institution wholly maintained out of State funds.

title: Protection of interests of minorities
text:
Constitution of India — Section 29: Protection of interests of minorities.
Any section of the citizens residing in the territory of India or any part thereof having a distinct language, script or culture of its own shall have the right to conserve the same.

title: Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions
text:
Constitution of India — Section 30: Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions.
All minorities, whether based on religion or language, shall have the right to establish and administer educational institutions of their choice.

title: Remedies for enforcement of rights
text:
Constitution of India — Section 32: Remedies for enforcement of rights.
The right to move the Supreme Court by appropriate proceedings for the enforcement of the rights conferred by this Part is guaranteed.

title: Uniform civil code for the citizens
text:
Constitution of India — Section 44: Uniform civil code for the citizens.
The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India.

title: Fundamental Duties
text:
Constitution of India — Section 51A: Fundamental Duties.
It shall be the duty of every citizen of India to abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem; to cherish and follow the noble ideals which inspired our national struggle for freedom; to uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India; to defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so; to promote harmony and the spirit of common brotherhood; to value and preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture; to protect and improve the natural environment; to develop scientific temper and humanism; to safeguard public property; and to strive towards excellence in all spheres of individual and collective activity.

title: Power of High Courts to issue certain writs
text:
Constitution of India — Section 226: Power of High Courts to issue certain writs.
Every High Court shall have power to issue to any person or authority, including in appropriate cases any Government, directions, orders or writs, including writs in the nature of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari, for the enforcement of any of the rights conferred by Part III and for any other purpose.

title: Special provisions with respect to Delhi
text:
Constitution of India — Section 239AA: Special provisions with respect to Delhi.
The Union Territory of Delhi shall be called the National Capital Territory of Delhi and shall have a Legislative Assembly with powers and functions as provided in this article.

title: Power of Parliament to legislate with respect to a matter in the State List in the national interest
text:
Constitution of India — Section 249: Power of Parliament to legislate with respect to a matter in the State List in the national interest.
Notwithstanding anything in the foregoing provisions of this Chapter, if the Council of States has declared by resolution supported by not less than two-thirds of the members present and voting that it is necessary or expedient in the national interest, Parliament may make laws with respect to any matter enumerated in the State List.

title: Provisions in case of failure of constitutional machinery in States (President’s Rule)
text:
Constitution of India — Section 356: Provisions in case of failure of constitutional machinery in States (President’s Rule).
If the President, on receipt of a report from the Governor of a State or otherwise, is satisfied that a situation has arisen in which the government of the State cannot be carried on in accordance with the provisions of this Constitution, he may declare that the powers of the legislature and the executive of the State shall vest in the President.

title: Provisions as to Financial Emergency
text:
Constitution of India — Section 360: Provisions as to Financial Emergency.
If the President is satisfied that a situation has arisen whereby the financial stability or credit of India or any part thereof is threatened, he may by Proclamation declare a Financial Emergency.

title: Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution
text:
Constitution of India — Section 368: Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution.
An amendment of this Constitution may be initiated only by the introduction of a Bill for the purpose in either House of Parliament, and when the Bill is passed in each House by a majority of the total membership of that House and by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the members present and voting.

title: Temporary provisions with respect to the State of Jammu and Kashmir (Historical)
text:
Constitution of India — Section 370: Temporary provisions with respect to the State of Jammu and Kashmir (Historical).
Notwithstanding anything in this Constitution, the provisions of Article 238 shall not apply in relation to the State of Jammu and Kashmir. The State was given special status, though most of these provisions have now been abrogated.

title: Legislation to give effect to certain provisions of this Part
text:
Constitution of India — Section 35: Legislation to give effect to certain provisions of this Part.
Notwithstanding anything in this Constitution, Parliament shall have, and the Legislature of a State shall not have, power to make laws with respect to matters relating to preventive detention, and other laws in certain specific areas mentioned.

title: Prohibition of employment of children in factories, etc.
text:
Constitution of India — Section 24: Prohibition of employment of children in factories, etc..
No child below the age of fourteen years shall be employed to work in any factory or mine or engaged in any other hazardous employment.

title: Abolition of Untouchability
text:
Constitution of India — Section 17: Abolition of Untouchability.
Untouchability is abolished and its practice in any form is forbidden. The enforcement of any disability arising out of untouchability shall be an offence punishable in accordance with law.

title: Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour
text:
Constitution of India — Section 23: Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour.
Traffic in human beings and begar and other similar forms of forced labour are prohibited and any contravention of this provision shall be an offence punishable in accordance with law.

title: Separation of judiciary from executive
text:
Constitution of India — Section 50: Separation of judiciary from executive.
The State shall take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State.

title: Information in cognizable cases (FIR)
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 154: Information in cognizable cases (FIR).
Every information relating to the commission of a cognizable offence, if given orally to an officer in charge of a police station, shall be reduced to writing by him or under his direction, and be read over to the informant, and every such information shall be signed by the person giving it.

title: Police officer’s power to investigate cognizable cases
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 156: Police officer’s power to investigate cognizable cases.
Any officer in charge of a police station may, without the order of a Magistrate, investigate any cognizable case which a court having jurisdiction over the local area would have power to inquire into or try.

title: Procedure for investigation
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 157: Procedure for investigation.
If an officer in charge of a police station has reason to suspect the commission of an offence, he shall forthwith send a report to the Magistrate empowered to take cognizance of such offence and shall proceed to the scene to investigate the facts.

title: Police officer’s power to require attendance of witnesses
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 160: Police officer’s power to require attendance of witnesses.
Any police officer making an investigation may require the attendance of any person being within the limits of his station who appears to be acquainted with the facts and circumstances of the case.

title: Examination of witnesses by police
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 161: Examination of witnesses by police.
Any police officer making an investigation may examine orally any person supposed to be acquainted with the facts and circumstances of the case.

title: Recording of confessions and statements
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 164: Recording of confessions and statements.
Any Metropolitan Magistrate or Judicial Magistrate may record any confession or statement made to him in the course of an investigation or at any time afterwards before the commencement of inquiry or trial.

title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in 24 hours (Remand)
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 167: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in 24 hours (Remand).
Whenever any person is arrested and detained in custody, and it appears that the investigation cannot be completed within 24 hours, the Magistrate may authorize the detention of the accused person otherwise than in police custody.

title: Report of police officer on completion of investigation (Charge Sheet)
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 173: Report of police officer on completion of investigation (Charge Sheet).
Every investigation under this Chapter shall be completed without unnecessary delay, and as soon as it is completed, the officer-in-charge of the police station shall forward to the Magistrate a report in the prescribed form.

title: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 190: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates.
Any Magistrate of the first class, and any Magistrate of the second class specially empowered, may take cognizance of any offence upon receiving a complaint, police report, or upon information received from any person.

title: Prosecution of Judges and public servants
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 197: Prosecution of Judges and public servants.
When any person who is or was a public servant is accused of any offence alleged to have been committed while acting or purporting to act in discharge of his official duty, no Court shall take cognizance of such offence except with the previous sanction of the appropriate Government.

title: Examination of complainant
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 200: Examination of complainant.
A Magistrate taking cognizance of an offence on complaint shall examine the complainant and the witnesses present, upon oath, and the substance of such examination shall be reduced to writing.

title: Discharge
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 227: Discharge.
If, upon consideration of the record of the case and the documents submitted, and after hearing the submissions of the accused and the prosecution, the Judge considers that there is not sufficient ground for proceeding against the accused, he shall discharge the accused.

title: Framing of charge
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 228: Framing of charge.
If, after such consideration and hearing, the Judge is of opinion that there is ground for presuming that the accused has committed an offence, he shall frame in writing a charge against the accused.

title: Discharge in warrant cases
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 239: Discharge in warrant cases.
If, upon considering the police report and documents, and after hearing the accused and prosecution, the Magistrate considers the charge to be groundless, he shall discharge the accused.

title: Framing of charge in warrant cases
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 240: Framing of charge in warrant cases.
If, upon consideration, the Magistrate is of opinion that there is ground for presuming that the accused has committed an offence triable under this Chapter, he shall frame in writing a charge against the accused.

title: Bail in non-bailable offences
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 437: Bail in non-bailable offences.
When any person accused of a non-bailable offence is arrested or detained without warrant and appears before a Court, he may be released on bail if the Court is satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for believing that he is not guilty of the offence.

title: Anticipatory bail
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 438: Anticipatory bail.
When any person has reason to believe that he may be arrested on accusation of having committed a non-bailable offence, he may apply to the High Court or Court of Session for direction to be released on bail in the event of such arrest.

title: Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 439: Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail.
A High Court or Court of Session may direct that any person accused of an offence and in custody be released on bail and may impose any condition which it considers necessary.

title: Absence of complainant
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 249: Absence of complainant.
When the complainant is absent on the date appointed for hearing, the Magistrate may, in his discretion, discharge the accused or adjourn the hearing.

title: Non-appearance or death of complainant
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 256: Non-appearance or death of complainant.
If the complainant does not appear on the date of hearing, or if the complainant dies, the Magistrate may acquit the accused.

title: Person once convicted or acquitted not to be tried for same offence
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 300: Person once convicted or acquitted not to be tried for same offence.
A person who has once been tried and convicted or acquitted of an offence shall not be liable to be tried again for the same offence, except in cases where retrial is permitted under law.

title: Power to examine the accused
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 313: Power to examine the accused.
In every inquiry or trial, the Court may, for the purpose of enabling the accused personally to explain any circumstances appearing in the evidence against him, put such questions to him as it considers necessary.

title: Compounding of offences
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 320: Compounding of offences.
Certain offences may be compounded by the persons mentioned in the Schedule, with or without the permission of the Court.

title: Court to be open
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 327: Court to be open.
The place where any Criminal Court is held for the purpose of inquiring into or trying any offence shall be deemed to be an open Court, to which the public generally may have access.

title: Evidence to be taken in presence of accused
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 353: Evidence to be taken in presence of accused.
Except as otherwise provided, all evidence taken during the trial or other proceeding shall be taken in the presence of the accused, or when his personal attendance is dispensed with, in the presence of his pleader.

title: Language and contents of judgment
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 354: Language and contents of judgment.
Every judgment shall be written in the language of the Court and shall contain the point(s) for determination, the decision thereon and the reasons for the decision.

title: Order to release on probation of good conduct
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 360: Order to release on probation of good conduct.
When any person not under twenty-one years of age is convicted of an offence punishable with imprisonment of not more than seven years or when any woman is convicted, the Court may release them on probation of good conduct instead of sentencing them to imprisonment.

title: Appeals from convictions
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 374: Appeals from convictions.
Any person convicted on a trial held by a High Court or by a Sessions Judge or an Additional Sessions Judge may appeal to the High Court.

title: Appeal in case of acquittal
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 378: Appeal in case of acquittal.
The State Government may, in any case, direct the Public Prosecutor to present an appeal to the High Court against an order of acquittal passed by any Court.

title: Calling for records to exercise powers of revision
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 397: Calling for records to exercise powers of revision.
The High Court or any Sessions Judge may call for and examine the record of any proceeding before any inferior Criminal Court to satisfy itself as to the correctness, legality, or propriety of any finding, sentence, or order.

title: High Court’s powers of revision
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 401: High Court’s powers of revision.
In the case of any proceeding the record of which has been called for by itself or which otherwise comes to its knowledge, the High Court may exercise its powers of revision.

title: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents
text:
Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) — Section 125: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents.
If any person having sufficient means neglects or refuses to maintain his wife, unable to maintain herself, or his legitimate or illegitimate minor child, whether married or not, unable to maintain itself, or his legitimate or illegitimate child (not being a married daughter) who has attained majority, where such child is, by reason of any physical or mental abnormality or injury unable to maintain itself, or his father or mother, unable to maintain himself or herself, a Magistrate of the first class may, upon proof of such neglect or refusal, order such person to make a monthly allowance for the maintenance of his wife or such child, father or mother, at such monthly rate as such Magistrate thinks fit. Explanation: For the purposes of this Chapter, 'wife' includes a woman who has been divorced by, or has obtained a divorce from, her husband and has not remarried. The maximum monthly allowance shall not exceed five hundred rupees in the whole. If any person so ordered fails without sufficient cause to comply with the order, any such Magistrate may, for every breach of the order, issue a warrant for levying the amount due, or may sentence such person to imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month or until payment if sooner made.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Definition of domestic violence
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 3: Definition of domestic violence.
Any act, omission or commission that harms or injures or has the potential to harm or injure the health, safety, life, limb, or well-being of the aggrieved person constitutes domestic violence. This includes physical, sexual, verbal, emotional, and economic abuse.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Information to Protection Officer and police officer
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 4: Information to Protection Officer and police officer.
Any person who has reason to believe that an act of domestic violence has been, or is being, committed may give information about it to the Protection Officer or police officer.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Duties of police officers, Protection Officers and Magistrate
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 5: Duties of police officers, Protection Officers and Magistrate.
Upon receiving a complaint, the police officer or Protection Officer must inform the aggrieved person about her right to file an application for relief, right to free legal aid, right to file a complaint under Section 498A of IPC, and availability of shelter homes.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Duties and functions of Protection Officers
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 9: Duties and functions of Protection Officers.
The Protection Officer shall assist the Magistrate in discharging functions under this Act, maintain lists of service providers, make domestic incident reports, and ensure the aggrieved person receives required medical aid and legal aid.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Service providers
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 10: Service providers.
Service providers are registered voluntary associations or companies who can provide legal aid, medical facilities, counseling, or shelter to the aggrieved person.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Application to Magistrate
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 12: Application to Magistrate.
An aggrieved person or Protection Officer may present an application to the Magistrate seeking reliefs under this Act. The Magistrate must fix the first hearing within 3 days and dispose of the application within 60 days.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Right to reside in shared household
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 17: Right to reside in shared household.
Every woman in a domestic relationship has the right to reside in the shared household, whether or not she has any title or rights in the property.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Protection orders
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 18: Protection orders.
The Magistrate may pass protection orders prohibiting the respondent from committing any act of domestic violence, aiding or abetting violence, entering the aggrieved person’s workplace, communicating with her, or dispossessing her from shared household.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Residence orders
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 19: Residence orders.
The Magistrate may direct the respondent to remove himself from the shared household, restrain him from dispossessing the aggrieved person, or direct alternate accommodation.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Monetary reliefs
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 20: Monetary reliefs.
The Magistrate may direct payment of monetary relief to meet the expenses incurred due to domestic violence, loss of earnings, medical expenses, loss or damage to property, and maintenance.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Custody orders
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 21: Custody orders.
The Magistrate may grant temporary custody of any child to the aggrieved person and specify visitation rights for the respondent, if any.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Compensation orders
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 22: Compensation orders.
The Magistrate may order the respondent to pay compensation for the injuries, including mental torture and emotional distress, caused by acts of domestic violence.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Service of notice
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 13: Service of notice.
The Magistrate shall ensure that a notice of the application is served on the respondent and Protection Officer within a maximum of 2 days from the date of its receipt.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Power to grant interim and ex parte orders
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 23: Power to grant interim and ex parte orders.
The Magistrate may pass interim orders or ex parte orders based on the affidavit of the aggrieved person if immediate protection is required.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Duration and alteration of orders
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 25: Duration and alteration of orders.
Orders passed under this Act shall remain in force until revoked or modified by the Magistrate upon receiving application by either party.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Relief available under other laws
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 26: Relief available under other laws.
The aggrieved person may seek reliefs available under this Act in addition to any other legal proceedings she may have initiated under other laws.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Jurisdiction
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 27: Jurisdiction.
The Magistrate’s court where the aggrieved person resides or where the incident of domestic violence occurred shall have jurisdiction to entertain applications under this Act.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Procedure
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 28: Procedure.
Proceedings under Sections 12, 18 to 23 and offences under Section 31 shall be governed by the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, while the rest shall follow Civil Procedure as prescribed.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Appeal
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 29: Appeal.
Any person aggrieved by the order of the Magistrate may prefer an appeal to the Sessions Court within 30 days from the date of the order.

act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
title: Penalty for breach of protection order
text:
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act — Section 31: Penalty for breach of protection order.
Breach of a protection order or interim protection order shall be an offence punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine up to twenty thousand rupees, or both.

title: Definition of Dowry
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 2: Definition of Dowry.
Dowry means any property or valuable security given or agreed to be given directly or indirectly in connection with marriage by one party to a marriage to the other party or by the parents or any other person to either party to the marriage.

title: Penalty for giving or taking dowry
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 3: Penalty for giving or taking dowry.
Any person who gives, takes, or abets the giving or taking of dowry shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years, and with fine which shall not be less than fifteen thousand rupees or the amount of the value of dowry, whichever is more.

title: Penalty for demanding dowry
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 4: Penalty for demanding dowry.
If any person directly or indirectly demands dowry, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to two years and with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees.

title: Agreement for giving or taking dowry to be void
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 5: Agreement for giving or taking dowry to be void.
Any agreement for giving or taking dowry shall be void.

title: Dowry to be for the benefit of the wife
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 6: Dowry to be for the benefit of the wife.
Where any dowry is received by any person other than the woman in connection with whose marriage it is given, such person shall transfer it to the woman within a specified time, failing which the person shall be punishable with imprisonment or fine.

title: Cognizance of offences
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 7: Cognizance of offences.
No court shall take cognizance of any offence under this Act except on a police report or complaint made by the aggrieved person or her relatives or a recognized welfare institution. All offences under this Act are non-bailable and non-compoundable.

title: Offences to be cognizable for certain purposes
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 8: Offences to be cognizable for certain purposes.
The offences under this Act shall be cognizable for the purpose of investigation and arrest without warrant.

title: Burden of proof
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 8A: Burden of proof.
Where any person is prosecuted for taking or abetting dowry, the burden of proving that he has not committed the offence lies on him.

title: Dowry Prohibition Officers
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 8B: Dowry Prohibition Officers.
The State Government may appoint Dowry Prohibition Officers to ensure compliance with this Act and prevent dowry offences.

title: Power to make rules
text:
Dowry Prohibition Act — Section 9: Power to make rules.
The State Government may, by notification, make rules for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

title: Conditions for a Hindu marriage
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 5: Conditions for a Hindu marriage.
A marriage may be solemnized between any two Hindus, if the following conditions are fulfilled: neither party has a spouse living; neither party is incapable of giving valid consent; neither party is suffering from mental disorder; the parties are not within prohibited degrees of relationship; the parties are not sapindas of each other.

title: Restitution of conjugal rights
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 9: Restitution of conjugal rights.
When either the husband or the wife has, without reasonable excuse, withdrawn from the society of the other, the aggrieved party may apply for restitution of conjugal rights.

title: Judicial separation
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 10: Judicial separation.
Either party may seek judicial separation on grounds specified in Section 13. The marriage remains legally valid, but parties are not obliged to cohabit.

title: Grounds for Divorce
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 13: Grounds for Divorce.
A divorce may be granted on any of the following grounds: adultery; cruelty; desertion for two years or more; conversion to another religion; incurable unsoundness of mind; virulent and incurable leprosy; venereal disease; renunciation of the world; not heard alive for seven years. Additionally, a wife may seek divorce if husband has married again, committed rape, sodomy or bestiality, or failed to comply with maintenance orders.

title: Divorce by Mutual Consent
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 13B: Divorce by Mutual Consent.
Both parties may jointly file a petition for divorce by mutual consent if they have lived separately for one year or more and agree that the marriage has broken down irretrievably.

title: No petition for divorce within one year of marriage
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 14: No petition for divorce within one year of marriage.
No petition for divorce shall be presented within one year of marriage unless the case involves exceptional hardship or depravity.

title: Divorced person when may marry again
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 15: Divorced person when may marry again.
When a marriage has been dissolved and there is no right of appeal, or the appeal period has expired without an appeal being filed, or an appeal has been dismissed, either party may marry again.

title: Legitimacy of children of void and voidable marriages
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 16: Legitimacy of children of void and voidable marriages.
Children born of void or voidable marriages are deemed legitimate, but such legitimacy does not confer inheritance rights on property not belonging to the parents.

title: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 24: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings.
Either the wife or the husband may claim maintenance and expenses during the pendency of proceedings under this Act. The court may order the other party to pay maintenance, considering the income and financial capacity of both parties.

title: Permanent alimony and maintenance
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 25: Permanent alimony and maintenance.
The court may, upon passing any decree under this Act, order the respondent to pay permanent alimony and maintenance to the applicant for life or for a specified period, considering the income and property of both parties and their conduct.

title: Custody of children
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 26: Custody of children.
In any proceeding under this Act, the court may pass interim or final orders regarding custody, maintenance and education of minor children, considering their welfare.

title: Disposal of property
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 27: Disposal of property.
The court may make provisions regarding the disposal of property jointly owned by both parties at the time of passing any decree under this Act.

title: Appeals from decrees and orders
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 28: Appeals from decrees and orders.
All decrees and certain orders under this Act are appealable to the appropriate appellate court.

title: Void marriages
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 11: Void marriages.
Any marriage solemnized after the commencement of this Act shall be null and void if it contravenes any one of the conditions specified in clauses (i), (iv), and (v) of Section 5.

title: Voidable marriages
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 12: Voidable marriages.
A marriage shall be voidable and may be annulled by a decree if: the marriage has not been consummated due to the impotence of the respondent; consent was obtained by force or fraud; or at the time of marriage, the respondent was pregnant by another person.

title: Court to which petition shall be presented
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 19: Court to which petition shall be presented.
Every petition under this Act shall be presented to the District Court within the jurisdiction where: the marriage was solemnized; the respondent resides; or where the parties last resided together.

title: Application of Civil Procedure Code
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 21: Application of Civil Procedure Code.
Subject to the provisions contained in this Act, all proceedings under this Act shall be regulated, as far as may be, by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

title: Decree in proceedings
text:
Hindu Marriage Act — Section 23: Decree in proceedings.
In any proceeding under this Act, the court shall satisfy itself that there is no collusion between the parties, and that the petitioner is not taking advantage of his or her own wrong, before granting any relief.

title: Culpable homicide
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 299: Culpable homicide.
Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, commits the offence of culpable homicide. Explanation 1: A person who causes bodily injury to another who is labouring under a disorder, disease or bodily infirmity, and thereby accelerates the death of that other, shall be deemed to have caused his death. Explanation 2: Where death is caused by bodily injury, the person who causes such bodily injury shall be deemed to have caused the death, although by resorting to proper remedies and skilful treatment the death might have been prevented. Explanation 3: The causing of the death of child in the mother's womb is not homicide. But it may amount to culpable homicide to cause the death of a living child, if any part of that child has been brought forth, though the child may not have breathed or been completely born.

title: Murder
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 300: Murder.
Except in the cases hereinafter excepted, culpable homicide is murder, if the act by which the death is caused is done with the intention of causing death, or if it is done with the intention of causing such bodily injury as the offender knows to be likely to cause the death of the person to whom the harm is caused, or if it is done with the intention of causing bodily injury to any person and the bodily injury intended to be inflicted is sufficient in the ordinary course of nature to cause death, or if the person committing the act knows that it is so imminently dangerous that it must, in all probability, cause death or such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, and commits such act without any excuse for incurring the risk of causing death or such injury as aforesaid. Exception 1 (Grave and sudden provocation): Culpable homicide is not murder if the offender, whilst deprived of the power of self-control by grave and sudden provocation, causes the death of the person who gave the provocation or causes the death of any other person by mistake or accident. Exception 2 (Good faith exercise of right of private defence): Culpable homicide is not murder if the offender, in the exercise in good faith of the right of private defence of person or property, exceeds the power given to him by law and causes the death of the person against whom he is exercising such right of defence without premeditation. Exception 3 (Public servant acting in furtherance of public justice): Culpable homicide is not murder if the offender, being a public servant or aiding a public servant acting for the advancement of public justice, exceeds the powers given to him by law, and causes death by doing an act which he, in good faith, believes to be lawful and necessary for the due discharge of his duty. Exception 4 (Sudden fight without premeditation): Culpable homicide is not murder if it is committed without premeditation in a sudden fight in the heat of passion upon a sudden quarrel, provided that the offender has not taken undue advantage or acted in a cruel or unusual manner. Exception 5 (Consent of the deceased): Culpable homicide is not murder when the person whose death is caused, being above the age of eighteen years, suffers death or takes the risk of death with his own consent.

title: Punishment for Murder
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 302: Punishment for Murder.
Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 304: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
Whoever commits culpable homicide not amounting to murder shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment up to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Attempt to Murder
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 307: Attempt to Murder.
Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine; and if hurt is caused to any person by such act, the offender shall be liable either to imprisonment for life, or to such punishment as is hereinbefore mentioned. When any person offending under this section is under sentence of imprisonment for life, he may, if hurt is caused, be punished with death.

title: Attempt to commit culpable homicide
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 308: Attempt to commit culpable homicide.
Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge that, if it caused death, he would be guilty of culpable homicide not amounting to murder, shall be punished with imprisonment or fine or both.

title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 323: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt.
Whoever voluntarily causes hurt shall be punished with imprisonment up to one year, or with fine up to one thousand rupees, or with both.

title: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 324: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means.
Whoever voluntarily causes hurt by means of dangerous weapons or means shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 326: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons.
Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt by dangerous weapons shall be punished with imprisonment for life or imprisonment up to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Punishment for wrongful restraint
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 341: Punishment for wrongful restraint.
Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with imprisonment up to one month, or with fine up to five hundred rupees, or with both.

title: Punishment for Theft
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 379: Punishment for Theft.
Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Theft in dwelling house
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 380: Theft in dwelling house.
Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Punishment for Extortion
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 384: Punishment for Extortion.
Whoever commits extortion shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Punishment for Robbery
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 392: Punishment for Robbery.
Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Punishment for Dacoity
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 395: Punishment for Dacoity.
Whoever commits dacoity shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Dishonestly receiving stolen property
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 411: Dishonestly receiving stolen property.
Whoever dishonestly receives or retains any stolen property, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be stolen, shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 420: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property.
Whoever cheats and dishonestly induces any person to deliver any property to any person shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 498A: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty.
Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects her to cruelty shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Punishment for Rape
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 376: Punishment for Rape.
Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of not less than ten years, which may extend to life imprisonment, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Dowry Death
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 304B: Dowry Death.
Where the death of a woman is caused by burns or bodily injury or occurs under suspicious circumstances within seven years of marriage and it is shown that she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by her husband or his relatives for dowry, such death shall be called dowry death, and the husband or relative shall be punished with imprisonment for not less than seven years which may extend to imprisonment for life.

title: Punishment for Criminal Conspiracy
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 120B: Punishment for Criminal Conspiracy.
Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to six months or with fine or with both if the conspiracy is not related to an offence punishable with death, life imprisonment or rigorous imprisonment of two years or more.

title: Forgery
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 463: Forgery.
Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record with intent to cause damage or injury or to support any claim or to cause any person to part with property, or enter into any contract, commits forgery.

title: Using as genuine a forged document
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 471: Using as genuine a forged document.
Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document or electronic record which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document shall be punished in the same manner as if he had forged such document.

title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 186: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions.
Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three months, or with fine up to five hundred rupees, or with both.

title: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 353: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any public servant in the execution of his duty shall be punished with imprisonment up to two years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 506: Punishment for criminal intimidation.
Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Sedition
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 124A: Sedition.
Whoever by words, spoken or written, or by signs or visible representation, brings or attempts to bring into hatred or contempt, or excites disaffection towards the Government shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment up to three years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Waging war against the Government of India
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 121: Waging war against the Government of India.
Whoever wages war against the Government of India, or attempts or abets the waging of such war, shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of duty
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 186: Obstructing public servant in discharge of duty.
Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions shall be punished with simple imprisonment up to three months, or with fine up to five hundred rupees, or with both.

title: Causing disappearance of evidence
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 201: Causing disappearance of evidence.
Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, causes evidence of the commission of that offence to disappear shall be punished with imprisonment or fine or both.

title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 354: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty.
Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Definition of Rape
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 375: Definition of Rape.
A man is said to commit rape if he penetrates his penis to any extent into the vagina, mouth, urethra or anus of a woman, or makes her do so with him or any other person, under circumstances specified under this section.

title: Punishment for kidnapping
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 363: Punishment for kidnapping.
Whoever kidnaps any person from India or from lawful guardianship shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.

title: Punishment for Defamation
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 500: Punishment for Defamation.
Whoever defames another shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.

title: Rioting
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 146: Rioting.
Whenever force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly, or by any member thereof, in prosecution of the common object of such assembly, every member of such assembly is guilty of the offence of rioting.

title: Bribery
text:
Indian Penal Code (IPC) — Section 171B: Bribery.
Whoever gives or accepts gratification to influence a person to exercise electoral rights shall be punished with imprisonment up to one year, or with fine, or with both.

title: Conditions relating to solemnization of special marriages
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 4: Conditions relating to solemnization of special marriages.
Notwithstanding anything contained in any other law, a marriage between any two persons may be solemnized under this Act if the following conditions are fulfilled: neither party has a spouse living; neither party is incapable of giving valid consent; neither party is suffering from a mental disorder; the parties are not within degrees of prohibited relationship; and the male has completed 21 years and the female 18 years of age.

title: Notice of intended marriage
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 5: Notice of intended marriage.
The parties intending to marry shall give written notice to the Marriage Officer of the district in which at least one of them has resided for not less than thirty days immediately preceding the date of notice.

title: Marriage notice book and publication
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 6: Marriage notice book and publication.
The Marriage Officer shall keep all such notices in a Marriage Notice Book and publish them by affixing a copy in a conspicuous place in his office.

title: Objection to marriage
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 7: Objection to marriage.
Any person may object to the intended marriage on the ground that it contravenes one or more conditions specified in section 4. The Marriage Officer shall inquire into the objection within 30 days.

title: Procedure on receipt of objection
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 8: Procedure on receipt of objection.
On receiving any objection, the Marriage Officer shall conduct an inquiry and make a decision. If satisfied that the objection is valid, he shall refuse to solemnize the marriage.

title: Divorce
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 27: Divorce.
A petition for divorce may be presented by either party to the marriage on grounds such as adultery, desertion for two years or more, cruelty, incurable unsoundness of mind, venereal disease, conversion to another religion, renunciation of the world, or not being heard alive for seven years. A wife may also seek divorce if the husband has committed rape, sodomy, or bestiality.

title: Divorce by mutual consent
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 28: Divorce by mutual consent.
Both parties may jointly file a petition for divorce by mutual consent if they have lived separately for one year or more and agree that the marriage has broken down irretrievably.

title: Restriction on petitions for divorce within one year of marriage
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 29: Restriction on petitions for divorce within one year of marriage.
No petition for divorce shall be presented within one year of the marriage unless there are exceptional circumstances causing hardship.

title: Alimony and maintenance
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 31: Alimony and maintenance.
The district court may, on application, order the payment of permanent alimony or maintenance to either spouse, considering the income and conduct of both parties.

title: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 36: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings.
Either party may apply to the district court for maintenance during the pendency of legal proceedings, and the court may order the payment of such sums as it deems reasonable.

title: Custody of children
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 38: Custody of children.
In any proceeding under this Act, the court may pass orders regarding custody, education, and maintenance of minor children, prioritizing their welfare.

title: Void marriages
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 24: Void marriages.
Any marriage solemnized under this Act shall be void if it contravenes any condition specified in section 4, such as existing spouse, prohibited relationship, or incapacity to consent.

title: Voidable marriages
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 25: Voidable marriages.
A marriage may be annulled as voidable if: it has not been consummated due to impotence; consent was obtained by fraud or coercion; or if the wife was pregnant by another person at the time of marriage.

title: Registration of marriages celebrated in other forms
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 8A: Registration of marriages celebrated in other forms.
Marriages solemnized in other forms may be registered under this Act upon mutual consent of both parties and fulfillment of conditions laid down in section 15.

title: Appeals from decrees and orders
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 37: Appeals from decrees and orders.
Appeals against decrees and orders passed under this Act may be preferred to the appropriate appellate court within prescribed time limits.

title: Jurisdiction and procedure
text:
Special Marriage Act — Section 39: Jurisdiction and procedure.
All proceedings under this Act shall be governed by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and the jurisdiction shall lie with the district courts having territorial authority over the parties.
